embeds.title: [Morimens] Beyond the Tempest - H9-3 w/ Corposant + Tulu
embeds.description:
This stage is difficult in its own right, but the mechanics of it are fairly simple. 

The boss gains 80 temporary Reinforce when you cut through one of their 5 phase changes, and their intention changes to an action that gives them 50 permanent Reinforce.

The main gimmick is that, at the start of

Comp dependent
For the one you mentioned with Castor Pollux Horla.
Also who is the target for battery? Castor?
The question for every team when touching team building, touching WoDs, touching CoVs, is, “Who is the Keyflare Awakener?”
Without Keyflare, you will Rouse on turn 4
Which then you’ll be dead
Pollux would be.

Put “Dusk and Dawn” on Horla. See if “Incalculable Favor” can generate enough Keyflare with “Dusk and Dawn” on Horla. If not, then run “Life Drain” with Keyflare WoDs on Winkle. Another option is Erica exists, and could swap out Horla or Winkle. Erica would then be Keyflare Awakener.

The twins demolished arc 1 for me with some hiccups. I do have both at E3. But if you can only E3 1 unit, it'll be Pollux first since he needs it more. Castor is fine at E0. 

As for teammates, I use Winkle who is a standard character so you can get her from losses and the standard banner. As said Ramona works well too. Another is Erica who we get for free. Lastly, Horla, she's a limited but she got put in the standard banner and the chance of getting her is very low but if you get lucky, she does well with the twins too. 

Castor and Pollux are rerunning again on the 4th and 11th.
Castor also runs really well with characters that can deal multiple hits thanks to his Corrosion mechanic, seeing those rats melting bosses is just chefs kiss

Correct. If that's a limited awakener banner, your chance of getting a featured awakener is 33%. If you lose and get a Standard awakener, it becomes 50%. If you lose again, it's 100%. So at worst it's 90 pulls for one featured awakener.

thanks for answering tho

You pretty much have a perfect Murphy:Fauxborn lineup, is what people were saying, so as you get luminous cores, you can pull for her to run fauxborn (gmurphy) + caecus + tulu + miryam as your primary team - there is an additional effect where gmurphy buffs some of the other blue units, that takes effect later too.

Personally, I would recommend running Murphy, Caecus, Tulu, G.Murphy first - It's a bit more defensive, and will make arc 1 a bit easier, because base Murphy has a way to counteract a lot of the ritual stacks

Once you get more familiar with it all this will make sense and then you can swap over to Miryam and go ballistic

Oh, and because I was way too far into playing before finding out, if you click on the tentacle icon on the left above your team, you can swap between blue (balanced, and generate more) white (Defensive), and Red (more damage, but lose a tentacle)

What’s annihilation?
Ultra stuff. Lets you remove the left card from ultra space once per turn.
If you click the purple vortex next to Ultra turn bar, you can burn the left most card to get a draw 1 gain 1 arith
So you can delay ultra turn, or you can just draw an extra and gain an arith when needed
Oh, that sounds very useful indeed.
Done well, you can get a 3 turn setup on a boss buff round, to prep in safety, debuff, then ultra round <:whenlifegetsatyou:749033396025557063>

The early game is easy enough that you can play through with a single team without understanding most of the game's mechanics.  I played the same Aequor/Chaos team through chp 7, and I didn't even use the other tentacle stances.  It's not until Chp. 8 and D-Tide that I actually had to read what things do and learn to play other characters/factions.  But I like that the difficulty can be tackled by playing better, not just spending more money.

Tinct is largely to setup big turns with her E3 which makes her shield skill give 45%crit rate+crit dmg next turn(including ultra turn), and her exalt adding another 15%crit rate/dmg with also a bunch of arithmetica reduction. She's better for boss fights than trash fights though as you usually can't setup her stuff for that.
